title: SpaceX And Anthropic Plan To Go Public — Here's What Happened After The 10 Biggest IPOs In History
author: Piero Cingari
description: Bank of America analyst Michael Hartnett examined the 10 largest IPOs in history to determine whether mega-listings predict market performance. The analysis reveals mixed results: Alibaba and ICBC preceded major bull markets (labeled "rocket fuel" IPOs), NTT and Enel came before bear markets, Visa and AIA marked market tops, while Saudi Aramco, SoftBank, Meta, and GM showed no clear directional signal. As SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic prepare for public debuts, history suggests the cycle timing matters more than the IPO itself.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Alibaba's September 2014 IPO preceded a powerful bull market in Chinese equities, with the Shanghai Composite climbing 119% in nine months and 38% in 12 months following the listing, labeled as 'rocket fuel' IPO.
Visa's March 2008 IPO marked a market top, with the S&P 500 declining 34% nine months after and 43% lower at 12 months as the global financial crisis unfolded.
AIA's October 2010 listing preceded a market decline, with the Hang Seng down 22% one year after the IPO, classified as a 'toppy' signal.
Meta's May 2012 IPO showed no clear directional signal, with the S&P 500 drifting modestly higher with modest gains of 16% at 9 months and 25% at 12 months.
GM's November 2010 IPO produced no clear directional signal, with the S&P 500 showing flat to modest performance (0% at 9 months, 7% at 12 months).
SoftBank's December 2018 IPO produced no clear directional signal, with the Nikkei index showing minimal movement and modest gains of 10% at 12 months.

title: Notified and Hong Kong Investor Relations Association Join Forces to Advance IR in Greater China Region
author: Clear® Verified
description: Notified, a technology partner for investor relations and public relations, has joined the Hong Kong Investor Relations Association (HKIRA) as an industry partner. The collaboration aims to enhance the IR profession and advance the adoption of AI in the Hong Kong and Greater China region.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Alibaba is mentioned as one of the leading companies that Notified serves in the Hong Kong and Greater China region, but no specific information is provided about the company's performance or outlook.
